title: Commodities and Inflation Protection - Client 036
content: Commodity investments provide inflation hedging, portfolio diversification, and exposure to global economic growth, particularly in developing markets. Commodity Categories: Energy (crude oil, natural gas) driven by supply-demand imbalances and geopolitical factors. Precious metals (gold, silver) offering monetary hedge and industrial applications. Industrial metals (copper, aluminum) benefiting from infrastructure and renewable energy buildout. Agricultural commodities (wheat, corn, soybeans) responding to food security and biofuel demand. Investment Vehicles: Avoid direct commodity ownership due to storage and complexity. Utilize commodity-focused ETFs (DBC, GSG), commodity futures mutual funds, and equity exposure through natural resource companies. Energy allocation through integrated oil companies (ExxonMobil, Chevron) providing dividend income alongside commodity exposure. Gold Allocation: Dedicated 10% portfolio allocation to gold via physical bullion ETFs (GLD, IAU) serves as monetary insurance and volatility dampener. Silver offers industrial demand component beyond monetary functions. Agricultural Exposure: Farmland REITs and agricultural equipment manufacturers (Deere & Company) provide diversified agricultural exposure without direct commodity ownership. Strategic Allocation: For client-036's $2.8M portfolio, recommend 15% total commodity allocation: 6% energy, 4% precious metals, 3% industrial metals, 2% agricultural. Rebalancing quarterly maintains discipline avoiding performance-chasing. Expected correlation: Low to negative with traditional stocks/bonds, enhancing diversification benefits.
